summary: Matched additional mnmain menu code by replacing placeholders with C implementations for color selection, light-object color interpolation, and menu navigation/input handling. The PR also tightened mnmain.h types so the shared menu state stores a GXColor pointer instead of an int, added the required GX/lobj includes, and adjusted data padding after new data/code was recovered.

key_files.role: Main implementation changes: added static GXColor table, matched mn_8022BFBC, fn_8022C128, and mn_8022DB10, removed a dont_inline pragma around mn_8022C010, updated inline initialization to pass an argument to mn_8022BFBC, and resized data_pad.
Header/type updates: added dolphin/gx.h, changed mn_unk1.x14 from int to GXColor*, and updated mn_8022BFBC prototype from int mn_8022BFBC(void) to GXColor* mn_8022BFBC(int).

what_changed: Added five static GXColor objects named mn_804D4B50, mn_804D4B54, mn_804D4B58, mn_804D4B5C, and mn_804D4B60 with RGBA constants used by the menu lighting/color logic.
Implemented mn_8022BFBC(int arg0) as a switch over values 0 through 4 returning pointers to the corresponding static GXColor objects.

Removed the placeholder for fn_8022C128 and implemented an HSD_GObj callback that gets an HSD_LObj via GET_LOBJ, runs HSD_LObjAnimAll, computes the target color from mn_8022C010 and mn_8022BFBC, and interpolates an LObj color toward the target over mn_804A04F0.x12 frames.
Defined the global mn_unk1 mn_804A04F0 in mnmain.c near the newly matched code.

Implemented mn_8022DB10(HSD_GObj*) as menu input/navigation logic using mn_80229624(4U), lbAudioAx_80024030 audio feedback, mn_804D6BC8 state updates, gm_801A4B9C/gm_801A4B60 cancel handling, and wraparound selection movement that skips invalid entries via mn_80229938.

In mn_8022DB10, selecting with input bit 0x10 maps current mn_804A04F0.x2 values 0 through 4 to var_r31 values 1 through 5, resets selection state, destroys/updates GObj links, and conditionally creates a GObj process from mn_803EB6B0[var_r31].x10.
Changed data_pad size from 0xd5c - 0xc4c to 0xd5c - 0xcd4, reflecting that newly matched code/data consumed part of the previous padding area.
Updated mn_8022DDA8_inline so mn_804A04F0.x14 is initialized with mn_8022BFBC(temp_r29) instead of calling mn_8022BFBC with no arguments.
Added sysdolphin/baselib/lobj.h include to mnmain.c for LObj types/macros used by fn_8022C128.
Added dolphin/gx.h include to mnmain.h and changed mn_unk1.x14 to GXColor*, aligning the struct field with actual color pointer usage.
smart_moves: Recovered the real type of mn_804A04F0.x14 from use sites: it is compared as a pointer and dereferenced for r/g/b channels, so changing it from int to GXColor* made fn_8022C128 and initialization code type-correct.
Modeled the color constants as static GXColor data rather than ad hoc integer constants, which matches the pointer-returning behavior of mn_8022BFBC and clarifies the data layout.
Used a switch-return helper mn_8022BFBC(int) to centralize the mapping from menu category/index to target color, then reused it both during initialization and per-frame update.

The LObj color transition logic preserves integer division behavior and guards each channel update with a nonzero delta check before adding delta / remaining_frames, which is likely important for matching generated code.
The menu navigation implementation uses do-while loops for left/right movement so invalid options are skipped until mn_80229938 reports a selectable entry.
Adjusted padding after introducing new global/static data, avoiding stale fake data layout that would conflict with recovered symbols.
Removed the previous dont_inline pragma around mn_8022C010 once surrounding code had been matched, suggesting the function could now stand on its own without artificial inlining constraints.
decomp_lessons: When an unknown struct field is used as both a comparison target and a source for color channels, update the struct/header to the specific pointer type rather than keeping placeholder integer typing.
Matching menu visual effects may require adding the relevant baselib object header; here LObj access and animation required lobj.h in the C file, while GXColor required gx.h in the public header.
Static color tables can be recovered incrementally from code that returns addresses into .data; giving each address-backed color a symbol preserves layout and makes switch-based helpers simple.
If matching new functions reduces the amount of anonymous data padding, update the pad expression to match the new known symbol boundary.

Input-handling functions often encode state-machine behavior, audio cues, object teardown/creation, and wraparound selection in one routine; preserve branch structure and temporary variable shapes when matching.
Use precise callback signatures such as void (*)(HSD_GObj*) and HSD_GObjProc* when reconstructing process creation code, because flag assignment and proc setup depend on these types.
naming_conventions: Unidentified menu functions and globals retain address-based names such as mn_8022BFBC, fn_8022C128, mn_8022DB10, mn_804A04F0, and mn_804D4B50.
Structs with unknown semantics continue using placeholder names mn_unk1 and mn_unk2, with fields named by offsets such as x0, x2, x12, and x14.
OnFrame callback naming remains explicit for known lifecycle roles, as in mn_8022DD38_OnFrame.
Temporary variables in matched functions use decomp-style register-derived names such as var_r31, temp_r29, temp_r28, var_r27, and temp_r3_2.
Header guard convention for this file is MELEE_MN_MAIN_H.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(8) in mn_8022DB10, indicating stack padding was required to reproduce the target stack frame/layout.
Preserved switch statements for small integer mappings in mn_8022BFBC and mn_8022DB10 rather than collapsing them into arithmetic or table lookups.
Kept explicit u8 casts when comparing or assigning menu indices against temp_r29 - 1, which may be necessary for the original instruction sequence.
Used assignment inside an if condition for temp_r28 = mn_803EB6B0[var_r31].x10 before creating a GObjProc, preserving likely original control flow.
Maintained do-while loops for selection wraparound so the generated branch shape matches the original behavior of repeatedly skipping invalid menu entries.
Called GET_LOBJ(arg0) and HSD_LObjAnimAll before color interpolation in fn_8022C128, matching the expected object animation/update order.
Changed mn_8022BFBC from a no-argument placeholder prototype to GXColor* mn_8022BFBC(int), then updated the caller to pass temp_r29, avoiding mismatched call signatures.

review_feedback.text: The provided PR slice reports one issue comment but includes no human_text_excerpt, no review comments, and no review_feedback_examples, so no specific reviewer feedback can be extracted.

follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename mn_unk1 or document the semantics of mn_804A04F0 fields x0, x1, x2, x11, x12, and x14.
Search for future matches of mn_8022C068 and mn_8022C304 to understand the full LObj/light setup around fn_8022C128.
Check whether mn_8022BFBC color constants are later given semantic names related to menu categories or player colors.
Find PRs touching mn_803EB6B0 or mn_80229938 to clarify the selectable-entry table and valid-option filtering behavior.
Investigate whether the issue comment on PR 1812 contained merge/status automation only, since no comment text was included in this slice.
